description: The ML Inference Service P99 latency has risen from 38ms to 67ms as the user base grew from 300M to 400M DAU. Root cause: L2 Redis cache miss rate increased from 8% to 22% for user targeting profiles due to long-tail users with infrequent activity. Design a solution that returns P99 to under 45ms without increasing the JVM heap beyond 32GB and without relaxing the consistency guarantee on budget enforcement data.
context: Current state: L1 in-process cache TTL=60s (JVM heap, 32GB total), L2 Redis TTL=5min (hit rate dropped from 92% to 78%), feature store P99=180ms. Long-tail users (30% of DAU, 2% of requests) have features that fall out of both L1 and L2 between requests. Budget enforcement cache is a separate Redis cluster — its consistency model must not be touched. JVM heap is capped by ADR-006 at 32GB; increasing it triggers longer ZGC evacuation pauses that worsen P99 tail latency.

checks.text: Does the proposal keep the JVM heap at or below 32GB — explicitly avoiding an increase to accommodate a larger L1 cache?
Does the proposal read budget balances from Redis atomic counters directly, not from the L1 or L2 feature cache?
Does the proposal address the long-tail cache miss problem — for example via L2 TTL extension, probabilistic early re-caching, or off-peak pre-warming?
